foundations.subject: surgery
foundations.reason: Segmental liver anatomy determines cyst localization, surgical approach, and proximity to major hepatic veins and bile ducts

foundations.subject: surgery
foundations.reason: Liver abscess is the primary differential for cystic hepatic lesions; distinguishing hydatid from pyogenic/amoebic abscess guides management

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Solid-appearing hydatid cysts can mimic hepatic tumors on imaging; differentiation is critical before intervention

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Intrabiliary rupture of hydatid cyst causes obstructive cholangitis, a surgical emergency

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Cystic liver metastases (ovarian, neuroendocrine) enter the differential for hepatic cystic lesions

objectives: SU28.10: Describe the applied anatomy of liver. Describe the Clinical features,
MI8.1: Enumerate the microbial agents and their vectors causing Zoonotic
integration: This undergraduate-level roadmap connects Hydatid Cyst across foundational sciences and clinical applications.
